description: baseline hybrid-wgsl-heads b4 depth1 4-FEN smoke

asi.hypothesis: baseline for LC0 hybrid-wgsl-heads batchSize=4 batchPipelineDepth=1 using existing autoresearch.sh; no code change
asi.note: readback wait dominates total eval time; JS legal-prior prep overlap remains tiny

description: omit mappedPolicy arrays from evaluator search path

asi.hypothesis: Avoid Array.from(mappedPolicyF32) allocation in Lc0WebHybridEvaluator search path by returning empty mappedPolicy while computing priors from typed readback data.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric worsened from 11.21 to 11.54 ms/eval; readback wait also worsened, so any allocation saving is below benchmark noise or harmful.
asi.next_action_hint: Focus on actual readback bytes/backend path rather than mappedPolicy result allocation; consider measuring opt-in GPU legal-prior backend separately before changing defaults.

description: use subarray views for batched WGSL readback slices

asi.hypothesis: Replace per-slot Float32Array.slice copies with subarray views in finishWgslBatch to reduce CPU copying after readback.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric regressed badly to 15.22 ms/eval; readback wait increased, so this did not produce a reliable E2E gain on the fast workload.
asi.next_action_hint: Do not revisit slice-vs-subarray without a focused microbench; main bottleneck remains GPU completion/fence variability rather than this CPU copy.

description: unchanged rerun to estimate fast-loop noise

asi.hypothesis: No code change; rerun baseline workload to characterize benchmark noise after two discarded micro-optimizations.
asi.rollback_reason: Unchanged rerun was slower than best baseline (11.49 vs 11.21 ms/eval), confirming fast-loop readback timing has noticeable run-to-run noise.
asi.next_action_hint: Require clear primary improvements or repeated confirmation before keeping tiny changes; consider using LC0_AR_REPS=2 for promising candidates.

description: benchmark opt-in GPU legal-prior backend in arena fixed suite

asi.hypothesis: Expose fixed-suite arena legalPriorsBackend param and test opt-in gpu legal priors to reduce readback bytes from full mapped policy to compact legal output.
asi.rollback_reason: Despite reducing readback bytes from 7444 to 3084, primary metric worsened to 13.63 ms/eval and dispatch_count increased to 160; fence/GPU work dominated over byte reduction.
asi.next_action_hint: GPU legal-prior path needs deeper shader/fence redesign before E2E use; don't promote or use as fast-loop default. Consider documenting as negative evidence and focus on avoiding extra dispatch/work.

description: process WGSL batch readback from mapped range without full buffer copy

asi.hypothesis: Avoid copying the entire mapped WGSL batch readback ArrayBuffer before per-slot postprocessing; process the mapped range view and rely on finally unmap for lifecycle cleanup.
asi.why_keep: Primary improved 11.21 -> 10.12 ms/eval and readback wait dropped; implementation is small and checks passed.
asi.risk: Keeps readback buffer mapped through per-slot postprocessing; finally cleanup should unmap on success/error, but lifecycle smoke/auto-review should be run before finalization.

description: confirmation rerun of mapped-range readback optimization

asi.hypothesis: Rerun kept mapped-range readback optimization to estimate whether the prior 10.12 ms/eval result was stable.
asi.rollback_reason: Confirmation run was much slower (13.82 ms/eval), so the apparent improvement is not yet reliable in the noisy 4-FEN fast loop.
asi.next_action_hint: Before building on this change, use stronger repeated/median benchmark or targeted lifecycle smoke; consider updating autoresearch.sh to use REPS=2 or 3 if iteration speed allows.

description: two-rep confirmation of mapped-range readback optimization

asi.hypothesis: Use a two-repetition median to confirm the kept mapped-range readback optimization under a less noisy fast loop.
asi.rollback_reason: Two-rep metric was 12.08 ms/eval, worse than both baseline and the kept single-run result; improvement remains unconfirmed.
asi.next_action_hint: Treat mapped-range no-copy as suspect despite kept commit; either run stronger A/B manually before finalization or try structurally different readback reductions.

description: restore early unmap/full-copy readback for two-rep stability check

asi.hypothesis: Because the kept mapped-range no-copy result was not confirmed, restore the older copy-then-unmap path and run a two-repetition benchmark to test whether earlier unmap/lifecycle behavior is faster or more stable.
asi.rollback_reason: Two-rep primary metric regressed to 13.97 ms/eval; readback wait also rose to 11.63 ms, so restoring the full-buffer copy path is not a promising fix for the noisy kept change.
asi.next_action_hint: Continue with structurally different readback reductions rather than toggling copy/no-copy behavior; next candidate is split WDL/policy readback attribution or a compact policy/top-k readback path without extra dispatch.

description: pre-copy per-slot readback slices then unmap before legal-prior postprocess

asi.hypothesis: Keep the no-full-buffer-copy approach but copy each slot's policy/WDL slices immediately and unmap the readback buffer before JS legal-prior postprocessing, reducing mapped lifetime without restoring the whole-buffer copy.
asi.rollback_reason: Two-rep primary metric regressed to 14.03 ms/eval; readback wait remained high, so earlier unmap with per-slot pre-copy did not stabilize or improve the kept no-copy path.
asi.next_action_hint: Stop toggling mapped lifetime/copy placement; pursue readback shape changes or telemetry split (WDL vs policy) to distinguish fence latency from byte/copy cost.

description: compact JS legal-prior readback with per-legal-move buffer copies

asi.hypothesis: Avoid full mapped-policy readback for the JS legal-prior path by copying only each legal move's mapped-policy logit plus WDL into the readback buffer, preserving legal-prior semantics without the extra GPU legal-prior dispatch.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric worsened to 12.85 ms/eval even though reported readback bytes dropped from 7444 to about 143 and dispatch count stayed 159; many tiny copyBufferToBuffer commands and/or unchanged fence latency outweighed byte reduction.
asi.next_action_hint: Byte reduction alone is not enough even without an extra dispatch. Avoid per-legal-move copy-command gather; if compact readback is revisited, it needs an integrated shader/top-k design with fewer commands or a separate microbench first.

description: avoid Array.from allocation for three-value WDL readback

asi.hypothesis: Remove tiny Array.from(wdlF32) allocations in single and batched WGSL-head result handling by reading the three WDL values directly from the Float32Array.
asi.rollback_reason: Single-run primary metric was 10.1245 ms/eval, effectively tied but slightly worse than the kept 10.122 ms/eval best; the change is too small and noise-prone to keep.
asi.next_action_hint: Do not spend more iterations on tiny CPU allocation cleanups unless paired with stronger repeated evidence; focus on readback/fence attribution or larger structural changes.

description: combine WGSL readback sanity scans into one pass

asi.hypothesis: The hot WGSL result sanity checks scan mapped policy and WDL twice (nonzero and variation). Combine those checks into one pass while preserving the zero/uniform validation semantics.
asi.why_keep: Primary improved from the kept 10.12 ms/eval result to 9.86 ms/eval and checks passed; the code change preserves validation semantics while reducing CPU scanning after readback.
asi.risk: Single-run improvement may still be readback noise; run a confirmation iteration before building larger changes on this result.

description: confirmation rerun of combined readback sanity scans

asi.hypothesis: Rerun the kept combined sanity-scan optimization without code changes to check whether the 9.86 ms/eval result is stable under the noisy fast loop.
asi.rollback_reason: Confirmation run regressed to 13.04 ms/eval with readback wait at 10.68 ms, so the single-run improvement is not stable enough to treat as strong evidence.
asi.next_action_hint: Keep the small semantics-preserving scan consolidation for now but require stronger repeated/alternating evidence before finalization; avoid further CPU micro-optimizations based only on single fast-loop wins.

description: two-rep confirmation of combined readback sanity scans

asi.hypothesis: Use a two-repetition run to confirm the kept combined sanity-scan change under a less noisy workload before treating it as reliable.
asi.rollback_reason: Two-rep metric was 13.60 ms/eval, far worse than the kept 9.86 single-run result, so the apparent improvement is not confirmed.
asi.next_action_hint: Mark the scan consolidation as low-confidence despite being committed; finalization should either revert it or require stronger alternating evidence. Future iterations should focus on measuring/attributing fence latency rather than CPU scan micro-optimizations.

description: fuse value WDL dense2 and softmax WGSL dispatch

asi.hypothesis: Reduce one WGSL-head dispatch per evaluated position by fusing the value head dense2 projection and three-way WDL softmax into a single compute shader, preserving the WDL math and readback shape.
asi.rollback_reason: Dispatch count dropped from 159 to 158 and checks passed, but the primary metric was 11.24 ms/eval, worse than the kept best and effectively baseline-level; the extra specialized shader did not translate into a reliable E2E gain.
asi.next_action_hint: Do not assume small dispatch-count reductions overcome readback/fence variability. Next promising path is explicit WDL-vs-policy readback attribution or stronger repeated A/B before keeping kernel micro-fusions.

description: map only active WGSL batch readback bytes

asi.hypothesis: When the reusable WGSL batch readback buffer has grown larger than the current batch, map and expose only the active batch byte range instead of mapping the whole buffer capacity.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric was 11.30 ms/eval, worse than kept results and slightly worse than the baseline-level comparison; exact-range mapAsync/getMappedRange did not reduce the dominant fence/readback time on this workload.
asi.next_action_hint: Partial-batch map range is not a useful lever for b4. Continue with attribution experiments or larger structural reductions, not WebGPU mapping API micro-tuning.

description: skip full mapped-policy sanity scan in WGSL search path

asi.hypothesis: Remove the hot full mapped-policy nonzero/variation sanity scan from WGSL evaluation result handling while retaining WDL validation; this preserves search outputs but reduces one post-readback pass over the 1858 policy logits.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric was 10.92 ms/eval, better than baseline but worse than the kept best and not enough to justify weakening mapped-policy validation in the opt-in WGSL path.
asi.next_action_hint: If sanity-scan cost is revisited, prefer an opt-in/profile-only validation toggle or stronger repeated evidence; continue toward WDL-vs-policy readback attribution rather than removing correctness guardrails.

description: two-rep revert test for combined WGSL readback sanity scans

asi.hypothesis: Because the combined sanity-scan keep has weak confirmation evidence, temporarily revert it and run a two-repetition benchmark to test whether the older separate nonzero/variation scans are more stable.
asi.rollback_reason: Two-rep primary metric regressed badly to 15.52 ms/eval and readback wait rose to 13.34 ms; reverting the scan consolidation did not improve stability or E2E throughput.
asi.next_action_hint: Do not spend more iterations toggling scan consolidation. Treat readback/fence variability as dominant and focus on attribution or structural changes with stronger evidence.

description: single-allocation JS legal-prior normalization loop

asi.hypothesis: Rewrite legalPolicyPriorsFromCandidates to build the legal-prior objects once and normalize in simple loops instead of map/spread/reduce/map, preserving priors and sort order while reducing JS allocation after readback.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric regressed to 12.79 ms/eval and readback wait dominated; the JS legal-prior allocation cleanup did not produce a reliable E2E improvement in the noisy b4 workload.
asi.next_action_hint: Stop spending iterations on small JS legal-prior CPU cleanups unless telemetry shows legal-prior postprocess dominates; focus on readback/fence attribution or larger structural reductions.

description: two-rep unchanged confirmation of current WGSL readback path

asi.hypothesis: Run a two-repetition unchanged confirmation of the current kept WGSL readback path to avoid building on single-run fast-loop wins and quantify current readback/fence variability.
asi.rollback_reason: No code change; two-rep metric was 13.48 ms/eval, far worse than the single-run kept results, reinforcing that the fast workload is dominated by readback/fence variability and current keeps remain low-confidence.
asi.next_action_hint: Use stronger attribution or alternating A/B before finalizing low-confidence keeps; next code experiment should avoid CPU micro-tuning and target structural readback/fence changes only.

description: direct mapped-policy WGSL shader without full policy-logits matrix

asi.hypothesis: Replace separate 64x64 policy-logits plus mapped-policy WGSL dispatches with one direct mapped-policy shader that computes only the 1858 LC0 mapped outputs from Q/K, reducing dispatch count and avoiding the full policy-logits intermediate while preserving logits and readback shape.
asi.rollback_reason: Checks passed and dispatch_count dropped from 159 to 158, but primary metric regressed to 11.49 ms/eval; the direct shader's extra per-output dot products and/or fence variability outweighed the removed dispatch/intermediate.
asi.next_action_hint: Do not pursue direct mapped-policy fusion without timestamp/microbench evidence; focus on attribution of policy head GPU work versus readback fence, or stronger A/B for structural shader changes.

description: start WGSL readback mapAsync after JS legal-candidate prep

asi.hypothesis: Delay WGSL batch readback mapAsync until after JS legal-candidate prep to test whether eager mapAsync-before-CPU-prep adds contention or lifecycle overhead beyond the tiny overlap it hides.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric regressed to 12.38 ms/eval and readback wait remained dominant; delaying mapAsync did not improve the b4 path, so the existing eager mapAsync scheduling is preferable.
asi.next_action_hint: Keep eager mapAsync-before-prep. Future iterations should avoid map scheduling micro-toggles and focus on structural readback/fence attribution or stronger A/B for larger shader/readback changes.

description: pad WGSL batch readback stride to 256-byte alignment

asi.hypothesis: Pad each physical WGSL batch readback slot to a 256-byte stride to test whether aligned destination offsets reduce WebGPU copy/map overhead despite a small increase in mapped bytes.
asi.rollback_reason: The module crashed at load time because the padded head readback constant referenced WGSL_BATCH_READBACK_ALIGNMENT_BYTES before that constant was initialized; no benchmark data collected.
asi.next_action_hint: If retrying alignment, define the alignment constant before both padded readback constants or inline the alignment value; then rerun the same hypothesis.

description: pad WGSL batch readback stride to 256-byte alignment

asi.hypothesis: Pad each physical WGSL batch readback slot to a 256-byte stride so copyBufferToBuffer destination offsets and per-slot mapped ranges are aligned, testing whether alignment reduces WebGPU copy/map overhead despite slightly higher readback bytes.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric regressed to 13.38 ms/eval and readback wait rose; increasing readback bytes from 7444 to 7680 for 256-byte alignment did not improve b4 fence/readback behavior.
asi.next_action_hint: Do not revisit simple stride padding; alignment is not the bottleneck. Focus future work on attribution or more substantial readback/fence reductions.

description: pack WDL into mapped-policy buffer to reduce head readback copy commands

asi.hypothesis: Write the WDL softmax output into the tail of an enlarged mapped-policy buffer so the standard WGSL-head path can copy policy+WDL to readback with one copyBufferToBuffer per slot instead of two, preserving readback shape and search semantics.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric regressed to 12.73 ms/eval and readback wait stayed high; reducing per-slot copy commands did not overcome the added shader/buffer complexity or the dominant fence variability.
asi.next_action_hint: Do not pursue simple copy-command packing further. Focus on attribution or substantial reductions in GPU work/readback dependence rather than copy-command count micro-optimizations.

description: compact JS legal-prior readback with one GPU logits-gather dispatch

asi.hypothesis: Replace full mapped-policy readback for the JS legal-prior batch path with a single WGSL gather dispatch that copies only legal-move logits plus WDL to the readback buffer, preserving CPU legal-prior normalization and search semantics while reducing readback bytes.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric regressed to 12.96 ms/eval despite reducing readback bytes from 7444 to 1036; the extra dispatch and unchanged fence/map latency outweighed byte reduction, and readback wait remained dominant.
asi.next_action_hint: Avoid compact readback designs that add a dispatch unless timestamp evidence shows GPU work can be hidden. Next focus should be WDL-vs-policy/fence attribution or stronger no-code repeated baselines rather than more byte-only reductions.

name: LC0 WGSL-head readback b4 stronger loop

description: switch autoresearch loop to stronger 16-position 500ms 3-rep median baseline

asi.hypothesis: Improve experiment quality by changing the autoresearch default workload from noisy 4-position/250ms/1-rep screening to a 16-position/500ms/3-rep median baseline before making more code changes, reducing false keeps without changing search semantics or stable runtime defaults.
asi.next_action_hint: Use this stronger baseline for future keep/discard decisions, then prioritize benchmark-only attribution modes (no-readback queue completion, WDL-only, policy-only, full readback, sanity-scan toggles) to guide structural work.

description: five-rep unchanged confirmation of stronger benchmark baseline

asi.hypothesis: Before more code changes, run an unchanged LC0_AR_REPS=5 confirmation under the new 16-position/500ms workload to quantify remaining full-search variance and avoid false keeps from noisy 3-rep medians.
asi.rollback_reason: No implementation change and primary metric was worse than the new 3-rep baseline (12.50 vs 11.65 ms/eval); this is calibration data, not an optimization to keep.
asi.next_action_hint: Proceed with benchmark-only attribution modes rather than hot-path micro-optimizations; account for residual search-output variance when evaluating future changes.

description: exploratory scheduler sweep depth2 on stronger WGSL b4 workload

asi.hypothesis: Explore batchPipelineDepth=2 under the stronger 16-position/500ms/3-rep WGSL b4 workload as a scheduler/deferred-readback lane to test whether overlapping batches hides the command/fence floor. This is speed/quality exploration only, not parity-preserving promotion evidence.
asi.rollback_reason: The benchmark command failed before emitting metrics, likely due the default 120s per-runtime timeout under depth=2; no performance data collected.
asi.next_action_hint: If retrying scheduler sweeps, raise LC0_AR_TIMEOUT_MS (for example 240000) and consider one smaller screening point first; still treat batchPipelineDepth>1 as speculative semantics, not a keepable parity optimization.
asi.error: LC0_AR_PIPELINE_DEPTH=2 ./autoresearch.sh exited with code 1 after 122.3s and emitted no METRIC lines.

description: retry exploratory scheduler sweep depth2 with raised browser timeout

asi.hypothesis: Retry batchPipelineDepth=2 under the stronger 16-position/500ms/3-rep WGSL b4 workload with LC0_AR_TIMEOUT_MS raised to 240000 to determine whether the previous failure was only the default browser timeout. This remains speculative scheduler exploration, not parity-preserving promotion evidence.
asi.rollback_reason: The benchmark still failed before emitting METRIC lines after 242.6s, so depth=2 is too slow/hung for this stronger fixed-suite configuration or needs a separate smaller scheduler-sweep harness.
asi.next_action_hint: Stop retrying full-strength depth=2 in this loop. If scheduler exploration continues, use a separate small screening matrix or dedicated timeout-aware script; otherwise prioritize benchmark-only attribution modes at batchPipelineDepth=1.
asi.error: LC0_AR_PIPELINE_DEPTH=2 LC0_AR_TIMEOUT_MS=240000 ./autoresearch.sh exited with code 1 after 242.6s and emitted no metrics.

description: unchanged stronger-loop rerun after failed depth2 scheduler probes

asi.hypothesis: Run an unchanged 16-position/500ms/3-rep baseline after the failed full-strength depth2 scheduler probes to recalibrate stronger-loop variance before making more code changes or adding attribution modes.
asi.rollback_reason: No implementation changed; the marginal primary improvement versus the 11.65 ms/eval baseline is small and should be treated as benchmark variance, not a keepable optimization.
asi.next_action_hint: Do not treat tiny unchanged improvements as signal. Next iteration should either add/run benchmark-only attribution through autoresearch.sh or use a dedicated smaller scheduler-sweep harness rather than changing runtime hot paths.

description: add deferred-readback attribution preflight and run stronger-loop benchmark

asi.hypothesis: Add a benchmark-only deferred-readback attribution preflight to autoresearch.sh and run it before the normal stronger fixed-suite benchmark, to measure whether double-buffered deferred readback can hide the command/fence floor without changing the parity-preserving batchPipelineDepth=1 primary workload.
asi.rollback_reason: The fixed-suite primary regressed to 13.16 ms/eval, so this run is not a keepable optimization; the attribution data is diagnostic only.
asi.next_action_hint: Deferred-readback microbench shows a large speedup (1.43x) with matching best moves in fixture mode, suggesting fence overlap is promising, but full search depth=2 timed out. Build a smaller dedicated scheduler/quality harness rather than retrying full-strength depth2 in this loop.

description: unchanged stronger-loop rerun with attribution hook disabled

asi.hypothesis: Run the stronger 16-position/500ms/3-rep parity-preserving b4 workload with the optional deferred-readback attribution hook disabled, to recalibrate baseline variance after the diagnostic attribution run before making more runtime changes.
asi.rollback_reason: No runtime implementation changed and the primary metric regressed badly to 16.52 ms/eval, with fewer visits/evals per position and higher readback wait. This is calibration/noise evidence, not a keepable optimization.
asi.next_action_hint: Do not infer performance from single stronger-loop reruns. Continue toward dedicated benchmark-only attribution/scheduler harnesses, and avoid committing runtime hot-path changes without repeated evidence.

description: repeat deferred-readback attribution with longer microbench window

asi.hypothesis: Repeat the benchmark-only deferred-readback attribution preflight with a longer 24-iteration window to confirm whether double-buffered readback overlap consistently beats immediate readback before investing in a dedicated scheduler/quality harness. The normal parity-preserving fixed-suite benchmark still runs afterward for the primary metric.
asi.rollback_reason: The fixed-suite primary regressed to 13.88 ms/eval and the attribution hook is diagnostic, not a direct b4 depth1 optimization; do not keep based on this run.
asi.next_action_hint: Deferred-readback attribution again shows a material fixture-mode speedup (1.34x) with matching best moves, so the next high-ROI path is a dedicated smaller scheduler/quality harness rather than more readback micro-optimizations or full-strength depth2 retries.

description: small scheduler screening depth2 still times out

asi.hypothesis: Screen batchPipelineDepth=2 on a much smaller 4-position/250ms/1-rep fixed-suite workload with a raised browser timeout, to see whether deferred/scheduler overlap can be evaluated in a reduced harness after full-strength depth2 timed out. This is speculative scheduler exploration, not parity-preserving promotion evidence.
asi.rollback_reason: Even the reduced depth2 fixed-suite timed out after 240s before emitting metrics; the browser wait failed while looking for LC0_FIXED_SUITE_DONE. No performance data collected.
asi.next_action_hint: Stop using fixed-suite batchPipelineDepth=2 for scheduler exploration in this loop. Build a dedicated scheduler/quality harness around the existing deferred-readback fixture or diagnose why depth2 hangs before retrying.
asi.error: agent-browser wait timed out after 10531ms near the 240s script deadline; see /tmp/lc0_autoresearch/20260605_113115_18898_rep1.log

description: opt-in WASM input backend screening is non-comparable to JS-input primary

asi.hypothesis: Expose arena fixed-suite inputBackend plumbing and screen opt-in WASM input at b4/depth1 under the stronger 16-position/500ms/3-rep workload, as a separate scheduler/input-backend lane suggested by the attribution discussion. Stable defaults remain JS input.
asi.rollback_reason: Although the reported metric improved to 9.75 ms/eval, it used a different input backend than the current primary workload (WASM instead of JS), so keeping it would corrupt the JS-input b4 baseline and overclaim a non-comparable improvement.
asi.next_action_hint: This is strong evidence to start a separate input-backend or scheduler sweep experiment with its own config/baseline, or explicitly reinitialize autoresearch for WASM-input b4 before pursuing it further. Do not mix this result into the JS-input primary loop.

name: LC0 WGSL-head WASM input b4 scheduler lane

description: baseline opt-in WASM input WGSL-head b4 depth1 lane

asi.hypothesis: Reinitialize autoresearch as a separate opt-in WASM-input/scheduler lane for hybrid-wgsl-heads b4 depth1, add fixed-suite inputBackend plumbing, and establish a clean 16-position/500ms/3-rep WASM-input baseline without mixing it into the previous JS-input readback objective.
asi.next_action_hint: Now confirm the WASM-input baseline with a 5-rep rerun or begin batch-size sweep at depth1 (b2/b4/b8), keeping batchPipelineDepth>1 as speculative scheduler exploration only.
asi.why_keep: This is the first baseline for the reinitialized WASM-input lane; checks passed and stable defaults remain unchanged because the backend is opt-in via query/CLI plumbing.

description: five-rep confirmation of WASM-input b4 depth1 baseline

asi.hypothesis: Run a five-repetition confirmation of the newly initialized opt-in WASM-input b4/depth1 baseline before making batch-size or scheduler changes, to estimate stronger-workload variance and avoid overfitting to the first 3-rep baseline.
asi.rollback_reason: No implementation change; the five-rep confirmation was slower than the 3-rep baseline (9.21 vs 8.59 ms/eval), so this is calibration evidence rather than a keepable optimization.
asi.next_action_hint: Proceed to WASM-input batch-size diagnostics at depth1 (b2 then b8) or a targeted b4 code change; require repeated evidence for any apparent small improvement because baseline variance is at least ~7%.
asi.baseline_variance_note: Five-rep metric is about 7.1% slower than the initial 3-rep baseline; do not overclaim tiny b4 improvements.

description: WASM-input depth1 batch-size sweep b2

asi.hypothesis: Sweep opt-in WASM-input hybrid-wgsl-heads at batchSize=2 with batchPipelineDepth=1 to test whether smaller physical batches improve full-search throughput by reducing per-batch latency or scheduler pressure compared with the b4 lane baseline.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric was slightly worse than the b4 baseline (8.66 vs 8.59 ms/eval), within noise but not an improvement; b2 also roughly doubled readback_maps versus b4.
asi.next_action_hint: Continue the depth1 WASM batch-size sweep with batchSize=8, then compare b2/b4/b8 using repeated confirmation only if one clearly beats the noisy b4 baseline.
asi.readback_maps_vs_b4: b2 readback_maps 0.51 vs b4 baseline 0.27, suggesting smaller batches increase map pressure without improving E2E throughput.

description: WASM-input depth1 batch-size sweep b8

asi.hypothesis: Sweep opt-in WASM-input hybrid-wgsl-heads at batchSize=8 with batchPipelineDepth=1 to test whether larger physical batches reduce map pressure and improve full-search throughput compared with the b4 lane baseline.
asi.next_action_hint: Because the b8 improvement is modest and the five-rep b4 confirmation showed ~7% variance, run a 5-rep b8 confirmation before treating batchSize=8 as the preferred WASM lane setting or changing autoresearch defaults.
asi.why_keep: Primary metric improved from 8.59 to 8.34 ms/eval and checks passed; this is a configuration/scheduler finding, not a stable-default promotion.
asi.readback_maps_vs_b4: b8 readback_maps 0.15 vs b4 baseline 0.27, consistent with lower map pressure from larger physical batches.
asi.noise_caution: The observed ~2.9% improvement is smaller than recent b4 confirmation variance, so confirmation is required before building on it.

description: five-rep confirmation of WASM-input b8 depth1 sweep

asi.hypothesis: Confirm the modest b8 WASM-input depth1 sweep improvement with a five-repetition run before treating batchSize=8 as the preferred lane setting, because the b4 five-rep confirmation showed substantial variance.
asi.rollback_reason: The five-rep b8 confirmation was slower than the b8 three-rep keep and slightly worse than the b4 baseline (8.62 vs 8.59 ms/eval), so the earlier ~3% b8 win is not robust enough to build on.
asi.next_action_hint: Do not switch the lane default to b8 yet. Either run an alternating b4/b8 confirmation matrix or move to a more structural change/harness; b8 still has lower readback_maps, but E2E throughput is within noise.
asi.noise_caution: b8 readback_maps stayed low (~0.147), but primary throughput did not confirm versus b4 baseline.

description: five-rep b4 control after b8 confirmation

asi.hypothesis: Run a five-repetition b4 WASM-input depth1 control adjacent to the b8 five-rep confirmation to compare configurations under nearby browser/GPU conditions instead of overfitting to isolated 3-rep medians.
asi.rollback_reason: No implementation change; the b4 control was slower than the lane baseline (9.12 vs 8.59 ms/eval), so this is calibration evidence rather than a keepable optimization.
asi.next_action_hint: Adjacent five-rep controls suggest b8 (8.62) may beat b4 (9.12) despite not beating the original 3-rep baseline; consider one more alternating/confirmation point or update the lane default to b8 only after stronger evidence. Avoid smaller b2 because it doubled map pressure.
asi.comparison_note: Recent five-rep b8 was 8.62 ms/eval with readback_maps ~0.147; adjacent five-rep b4 was 9.12 ms/eval with readback_maps ~0.267.

description: WASM-input depth1 batch-size extrapolation b16

asi.hypothesis: Extrapolate the WASM-input depth1 physical batch-size sweep to batchSize=16 to test whether further reducing readback map pressure improves full-search throughput beyond the b8 and b4 points.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric worsened to 8.81 ms/eval versus the b4 lane baseline and b8 screen, despite readback_maps dropping to ~0.087; larger batches reduce map pressure but appear to hurt E2E search throughput/latency balance.
asi.next_action_hint: Do not continue increasing batch size. Treat b8 as the upper useful range and prefer an alternating b4/b8 confirmation or structural scheduler/readback harness rather than b16/b32 sweeps.
asi.auto_resume_limit_note: Created leelaweb-arena-diagnostics/autoresearch.config.json with maxIterations=100; the separate Pi auto-resume turn cap may still require user-side restart/continue if the harness stops after 20 turns.
asi.readback_maps_vs_b4_b8: b16 readback_maps ~0.087 vs b8 ~0.147 and b4 ~0.267, but lower map count did not improve primary throughput.

description: repeat WASM-input b8 depth1 after b16 sweep

asi.hypothesis: Repeat the WASM-input batchSize=8 depth1 point after b16 and adjacent b4/b8 controls to test whether the earlier b8 improvement is robust enough to justify changing the lane default or continuing batch-size tuning.
asi.rollback_reason: The repeat b8 metric was 9.00 ms/eval, slower than the b4 lane baseline and consistent with the failed five-rep confirmation; the earlier b8 keep is likely noise or at least not robust.
asi.next_action_hint: Stop batch-size sweeps for now: b2 increased map pressure, b16 hurt throughput, and b8 lowers map count but does not reliably improve E2E. Move to structural scheduler/readback harness or another attribution-driven change.

description: unchanged WASM-input b4 depth1 control after scheduler fixture crashes

description: WASM-input depth1 batch-size interpolation b6

asi.hypothesis: Interpolate the WASM-input depth1 physical batch-size sweep at batchSize=6: it may reduce readback map pressure relative to b4 without the latency/search-throughput penalty observed at b8/b16.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric regressed to 9.38 ms/eval versus the b4 baseline 8.59. Although readback_maps fell to about 0.19, readback wait and total eval time increased, so b6 has the same latency tradeoff pattern as larger batches.
asi.next_action_hint: Stop batch-size interpolation; b2 increased map pressure, b6/b8/b16 do not robustly beat b4. Return to depth1 readback attribution or inspect the scheduler/fixture liveness issue rather than more batch-size sweeps.

description: opt-in WASM legal-priors backend in WASM-input b4 lane

asi.hypothesis: Expose fixed-suite/arena plumbing for opt-in legalPriorsBackend and test legalPriorsBackend=wasm with the current WASM input, WGSL-head, b4/depth1 lane; WASM legal priors might reduce JS legal-prior postprocess without changing stable defaults.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric regressed to 9.04 ms/eval versus the 8.59 b4 baseline. The run eliminated reported legal_priors_prep_ms but readback wait increased and E2E throughput fell, so legal-prior CPU prep is not the limiting lever in this lane.
asi.next_action_hint: Do not pursue WASM legal priors for b4/depth1 unless telemetry changes. Return to readback/fence attribution or debug scheduler fixture liveness; keep JS legal priors as the lane default.

description: opt-in mixed-tvm-ffn encoder kernel for WASM-input WGSL-head b4

asi.next_action_hint: The mixed-tvm-ffn screen produced a large primary improvement; run a 5-rep confirmation and/or adjacent hand-kernel control before treating it as robust, because this lane has shown noisy single-run wins.
asi.cleanup_note: Removed the unused broken scheduler fixture preflight block from autoresearch.sh before keeping; retained useful opt-in attribution input-backend and encoder-kernel plumbing.

description: five-rep confirmation of mixed-tvm-ffn WASM-input b4

asi.hypothesis: Run a five-repetition confirmation of the opt-in mixed-tvm-ffn encoder kernel with WASM input, WGSL heads, batchSize=4, depth1 to test whether the prior 7.60 ms/eval screen is robust under stronger repetition.
asi.rollback_reason: No implementation change and the confirmation metric (8.00 ms/eval) was slower than the prior mixed-tvm-ffn keep (7.60), so this is confirmation/calibration evidence rather than a new keep. It still remains materially faster than the hand-kernel b4 baseline around 8.59 ms/eval.
asi.next_action_hint: Mixed-tvm-ffn still looks promising but noisy. Run an adjacent 5-rep hand-kernel control or test the other opt-in TVM variants (mixed-tvm-ffn-outproj, tvm-packed-f16) before changing lane defaults or overclaiming.

description: adjacent five-rep hand-kernel control for mixed-tvm-ffn comparison

asi.rollback_reason: No implementation change and the hand-kernel control regressed to 9.95 ms/eval, so this is calibration evidence rather than a keepable optimization. Adjacent evidence strengthens the case that mixed-tvm-ffn is materially faster than hand under current conditions, though both are noisy.
asi.next_action_hint: Continue the opt-in encoder-kernel matrix: test mixed-tvm-ffn-outproj and/or tvm-packed-f16 with the same WASM-input b4/depth1 workload before deciding which TVM variant is preferred. Keep defaults unchanged.
asi.comparison_note: hand 5-rep control (9.95 ms/eval) is much slower than adjacent mixed-tvm-ffn 5-rep confirmation (8.00 ms/eval)

description: WASM-input b4 opt-in mixed-tvm-ffn-outproj encoder kernel screen

asi.hypothesis: Screen the opt-in mixed-tvm-ffn-outproj encoder kernel with WASM input, WGSL heads, batchSize=4, depth1 to see whether adding TVM-packed attention output projection on top of mixed-tvm-ffn improves the current mixed-kernel candidate.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric was 7.77 ms/eval, which is faster than the original hand-kernel baseline but slower than the current mixed-tvm-ffn keep at 7.60 ms/eval. The added out-projection TVM kernel did not clearly improve the current best candidate.
asi.next_action_hint: Prefer mixed-tvm-ffn over mixed-tvm-ffn-outproj unless later repeated evidence contradicts this. Next run should test tvm-packed-f16 or run another mixed-tvm-ffn confirmation/32-position check before promotion claims.

description: WASM-input b4 opt-in tvm-packed-f16 encoder kernel screen

asi.hypothesis: Screen the opt-in tvm-packed-f16 encoder kernel with WASM input, WGSL heads, batchSize=4, depth1 to test whether fully packed/generated f16 kernels improve over the current mixed-tvm-ffn candidate.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric was 7.63 ms/eval, faster than the original hand-kernel baseline but slightly worse than the current mixed-tvm-ffn keep at 7.60 ms/eval. It also increased dispatch_count from 160 to 180, so the full packed-f16 path is not clearly preferable for this workload.
asi.next_action_hint: Prefer mixed-tvm-ffn over tvm-packed-f16 and mixed-tvm-ffn-outproj for now. Next iteration should confirm mixed-tvm-ffn again, try mixed-tvm-ffn at batchSize=8, or run a stronger 32-position/5-rep check before any promotion claims.

description: mixed-tvm-ffn WASM-input depth1 batch-size b8 screen

asi.hypothesis: Combine the current opt-in mixed-tvm-ffn encoder kernel with batchSize=8 at depth1 under WASM input/WGSL heads. Since b8 reduces readback map pressure, the faster encoder might make larger physical batches beneficial despite prior hand-kernel b8 noise.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric was 7.69 ms/eval, faster than the old hand-kernel b4 baseline but slower than the current mixed-tvm-ffn b4 keep at 7.60 ms/eval. Lower readback_maps (~0.15) did not beat b4 E2E throughput.
asi.next_action_hint: Keep mixed-tvm-ffn at batchSize=4 as the preferred candidate. Avoid further batch-size sweeps unless paired with a stronger alternating matrix; next useful work is stronger 32-position or 5-rep mixed-tvm-ffn confirmation before promotion claims.

description: 32-position stronger check for mixed-tvm-ffn WASM-input b4

asi.rollback_reason: This used LC0_AR_MAX_POSITIONS=32, which is stronger diagnostic evidence but not the default primary workload/baseline, and no implementation changed; log as calibration rather than a new keep despite the excellent metric.
asi.next_action_hint: Mixed-tvm-ffn remains strongly promising on 32 positions. Next, run an adjacent 32-position hand-kernel control or a 32-position ONNX b1 comparison before documenting/promotion claims; keep stable defaults unchanged.

description: 32-position hand-kernel control for mixed-tvm-ffn stronger check

asi.rollback_reason: No implementation changed and this 32-position hand-kernel control is diagnostic/non-comparable to the default 16-position primary baseline. It is slower than the adjacent 32-position mixed-tvm-ffn result (8.83 vs 6.89 ms/eval), strengthening mixed-tvm-ffn evidence but not a new optimization to keep.
asi.next_action_hint: Mixed-tvm-ffn is strongly favored over hand on adjacent 32-position evidence. Next, run a stronger ONNX b1 comparison or update documentation/notes only after one more confirmation; stable defaults must remain unchanged.

description: default 16-position mixed-tvm-ffn confirmation after stronger controls

It remains substantially faster than hand-kernel default controls and the original WASM-input b4 baseline.

description: mixed-tvm-ffn WASM-input depth1 batch-size b2 screen

asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric was 7.95 ms/eval, slower than the current mixed-tvm-ffn b4 keep at 7.60 ms/eval and slower than recent default confirmations. Smaller b2 roughly doubled readback_maps to ~0.51 and did not improve E2E throughput.
asi.next_action_hint: Keep mixed-tvm-ffn at batchSize=4 as the preferred candidate; b2 and b8 both failed to beat b4. Next useful iteration should be documentation/lifecycle validation or a stronger alternating confirmation, not more batch-size sweeps.

The faster encoder may make the compact legal-prior readback worthwhile by reducing readback bytes from full mapped policy despite one extra dispatch.

The result remains faster than the 8.00 ms/eval mixed-tvm-ffn JS-legal 5-rep confirmation while preserving the readback byte reduction to 3084.

asi.rollback_reason: No implementation changed and the JS-legal control metric (8.17 ms/eval) was slower than both the adjacent GPU-legal five-rep confirmation (7.71 ms/eval) and the current GPU-legal keep (7.50 ms/eval), so this is calibration/control evidence rather than a new improvement.
asi.next_action_hint: GPU legal priors plus mixed-tvm-ffn now has adjacent five-rep evidence over JS legal, but the margin is modest/noisy. Next run should use a 32-position confirmation for mixed-tvm-ffn + GPU legal priors or perform targeted browser lifecycle validation before documentation/finalization; keep all options opt-in and stable defaults unchanged.

The 32-position GPU-legal result (7.82 ms/eval) was slower than the earlier 32-position mixed-tvm-ffn JS-legal result (6.89 ms/eval), so GPU legal priors should not be treated as clearly preferred despite the default-workload keep.

The JS-legal 32-position control (7.66 ms/eval) was faster than the adjacent GPU-legal 32-position run (7.82 ms/eval), so GPU legal priors are not clearly preferred on the stronger corpus despite reducing readback bytes.
asi.next_action_hint: Favor opt-in mixed-tvm-ffn with JS legal priors as the more robust candidate, while keeping GPU legal priors as an opt-in low-confidence keep. asi.rollback_reason: No implementation changed and the default JS-legal control regressed to 8.53 ms/eval, slower than the mixed-tvm-ffn JS-legal keep/confirmations and slower than the current GPU-legal keep at 7.50 ms/eval. This is calibration evidence, not a new optimization.
asi.next_action_hint: Do not infer a preference from this noisy single control alone. If continuing comparisons, run an adjacent default GPU-legal control under the same branch or move to readback-matrix attribution/lifecycle validation; keep GPU legal and TVM paths opt-in and stable defaults unchanged.

asi.next_action_hint: Do not promote or prioritize batchPipelineDepth=2 b2 for fixed-suite throughput. If scheduler work continues, keep it in a dedicated fixed-visit/readback matrix lane; for parity-preserving optimization return to depth1 mixed-tvm-ffn JS/GPU legal comparisons, lifecycle validation, or documentation updates.

description: fixed-suite depth2 b4 check after readback-matrix pipe2 signal

Total eval/mapAsync timing also roughly doubled, so the matrix overlap signal did not produce a robust full-search win.

Return to depth1 mixed-tvm-ffn JS/GPU legal evidence, readback-matrix attribution, lifecycle validation, or documentation updates rather than more depth2 fixed-suite retries.

Favor mixed-tvm-ffn with JS legal as the more robust depth1 candidate, or move to lifecycle/docs/readback-matrix attribution rather than more single-run GPU-legal toggles.

This is calibration/noise evidence rather than an optimization; readback wait rose materially despite unchanged readback bytes and dispatch count.
asi.next_action_hint: Current browser/GPU conditions appear noisy or degraded. Before drawing further conclusions, run a plain hand-kernel or mixed-tvm-ffn 3-rep control, or inspect artifacts/logs for lifecycle/readback regressions. Avoid adding new micro-optimizations based on this noisy window.
asi.noise_note: Both recent GPU-legal and JS-legal depth1 controls regressed substantially; readback wait dominated, suggesting environmental variance or degraded browser/GPU state rather than a semantic change.

description: hand-kernel depth1 control to diagnose degraded browser/GPU window

This supports the diagnosis that current browser/GPU conditions are degraded/noisy rather than an actionable optimization signal.

Consider clearing browser/GPU state more aggressively, inspecting artifacts/logs, running a short readback matrix/control after cooldown, or documenting noise; avoid hot-path micro-optimizations based on these inflated readback waits.
asi.noise_note: Recent JS-legal mixed, GPU-legal mixed, and now hand controls all show large readback-wait inflation, indicating environmental variance/degraded WebGPU state.

description: post-cleanup mixed-tvm-ffn JS-legal control in degraded window
